$purpose: Tree representation of all /wb* commands with full dependencies, flags, chains, recommended models, prerequisites, and refusal conditions. Read this before running any /wb* command in chains.

$flag_matrix_legend.--act: Routes the source command's output through the /wbActOn engine — produces a ranked execution thread (action file).
$flag_matrix_legend.--wbPlan: Routes the source command's output through the /wbActOn plan-generation phase — produces a sibling plan file with worker/validator task tables for 🔵 multi-step findings.
$flag_matrix_legend.--act --wbPlan: Composable. Produces source output + action file + plan file in one run.

$smart_merge_protocol.description: When a second model appends to an existing daily file for a finding-based command, it MUST scan for duplicate findings and build a Consensus Table instead of blindly appending. First model writes normally; second model promotes to consensus.

consensus_rules.worker: Unanimous → use it. Code-heavy → pick throughput model. Reasoning-heavy → pick depth model. Tie → first-mover.
consensus_rules.validator: Pick the model NOT chosen as worker (independent perspective).
consensus_rules.severity: When models disagree, use the HIGHER severity (conservative).
consensus_rules.confidence: 🟢 N/N = all agree, 🟡 K/N = partial, 🔴 1/N = single model only.

$unified_plan_backlog.description: The plan file is the single source of truth for ALL work items. Commands that detect issues auto-send findings to the plan file alongside their normal report output.

recursive_tasks.description: When a Task cell contains a /wb* command, executing it spawns a sub-plan appended to the same file.

$model_recommendation_legend.sonnet_4: Fast, cheap. Best for one-shot inline edits, simple lookups, formatting.
$model_recommendation_legend.opus_4: Reasoning-heavy. Best for triage, refactor judgment, strategic memos, plan decomposition.
$model_recommendation_legend.qwen3_coder: Code-throughput. Best for sweeps, mechanical refactors, scaffolding.
$model_recommendation_legend.gemini_3_1_pro: Long-form synthesis. Alternative to Opus for strategic / planning work.
$model_recommendation_legend.gemini_flash: High-throughput cheap. Best for repetitive jobs, batch translations.
$model_recommendation_legend.deepseek_r1: Validation. Best as a worker/validator pair counterpart to Claude or Qwen3.

wbSetup.description: Initializes the Agentic Brain for the target folder by establishing its agentic identity.
wbSetup.role: The Architect — bootstraps a new package's agentic context layer.
wbSetup.belt: Black — used rarely; only when adding a new package

example.description: Bootstrap wb-core's agentic context + dev rules.
Bootstrap with deep focus on a sub-system.

outputs.content: Environmental Discovery, Core Workflow Files, Global Synchronization, Audit Trail
Local context (created if missing)
Local dev rules (created if missing)

prerequisites: git working tree should be clean (recommended)
refuses_when: target already has a wbSetup marker (re-run requires explicit --force)
notSuitableFor: Adding rules to an already-bootstrapped package (use direct edits to dev.md instead)

wbContext.description: Generates a comprehensive Context Report (identity, dependencies, constraints).
wbContext.role: The Synchronizer — catches up the AI on the package's current state.

outputs.content: Identity, Dependencies, Constraints

example.description: Context for a single entry point.

outputs.content: Identity, Dependencies, Constraints specific to the file

prerequisites: ideally a prior /wbSetup, but not required

wbPlan.description: Creates a structured multi-agent task plan for a given problem or objective.
wbPlan.role: The General — coordinates workers and validators.
wbPlan.phase: Midday (when work is bigger than one sentence)

input.content: Context files, past audit/review reports, optional user description

outputs.content: Task table with worker/validator assignments, checkboxes (⬜/✅), scoring, multi-agent SmartPrompt header.

feeds_into: wbReview (when plan is reviewed against execution)

refuses_when: target has zero source files (nothing to plan)
notSuitableFor: Tasks describable in one sentence (just describe; skip /wbPlan)

wbAudit.description: Performs a deep, honest, code-based technical audit.

input.content: All code and architecture within the folder, prior audit reports for temporal memory

outputs.content: 9-section audit report with tables, concrete evidence, score out of 10, status (BETA/PROD).

example.description: Audit a single doc/file for missing points + comparison table.

outputs.content: File-level analysis + enhanced suggestion + comparison table.

fed_by: wbStandup (recommends running audit on flagged packages)

prerequisites: recent /wbContext on the same target (otherwise audits last-week's understanding)

wbReview.description: Performs a formal quality review of an executed task against its original plan.
wbReview.role: The Inspector — surgical Plan vs Reality check.
wbReview.belt: Black — used after Path-B multi-agent loops

example.description: Review wb-press2 implementation against its plan.

input.content: Current codebase + the ORIGINAL plan markdown file

outputs.content: Plan vs Reality, Technical Integrity, Score (1-10), PASS/FAIL.

prerequisites: the plan file to review against must exist
execution work has happened (otherwise review is trivially FAIL)
refuses_when: plan file path is invalid
no implementation work to review
notSuitableFor: PR-level review without a plan (use a manual code review)

wbTest.description: Executes dynamic tests (UNIT, E2E, MANUAL, PERF) to verify code behavior.
wbTest.role: The Tester — runs the existing test suites.

example.description: E2E manual testing on the demo app.

outputs.content: Test setup, execution logs, verification results, PASS/FAIL.

refuses_when: no test suite found (suggest creating one first)

wbRelease.description: Orchestrates the release: resolves circular workspace deps, bumps versions across the monorepo.
wbRelease.role: The Release Engineer — monorepo-wide version coordinator.

outputs.content: CHANGELOG, topological build order, unlinking strategy.

refuses_when: recent /wbTest report shows failures
uncommitted changes exist
notSuitableFor: Single-package release (use /wbPublish on the package directly)

wbPublish.description: Executes the final build and NPM deployment based on the wbRelease plan.
wbPublish.role: The Shipper — pushes packages to npm.

input.content: Most recent release_*.md, prepared package.json files

outputs.content: Publish log, npm dry-run output, errors if any.

refuses_when: no recent /wbRelease (publishing without bump = re-publishing same tarball)
/wbSecure has CRITICAL findings
dist/ vs dist-dev/ mismatch in package.json

wbDeploy.description: Builds and deploys an app (e.g. to GitHub Pages, Vercel, custom host).
wbDeploy.role: The Shipper (apps) — pushes apps to the live web.

example.description: Deploy demo app to the live web.
Serve the prod build locally for sanity check.

outputs.content: Deploy log, URL, base-url verification.

refuses_when: recent /wbTest failed
/wbSecure has CRITICAL findings
app has handling user input AND no recent /wbSecure
notSuitableFor: Hot-fix without test (build a tiny patch app instead)

wbClean.description: Finds dead code, unused files, console.log, commented-out blocks.

outputs.content: List of files to delete, blocks to remove, with reasons.

feeds_into: wbAudit (clean before audit, but never refactor before audit)

refuses_when: target has no source files

wbLicense.description: Per-component license/tier gate injection on an already-monetized package.

example.description: Add Pro-tier gate to AdvancedFilter component.

prerequisites: package has wbMonetize marker (run /wbMonetize first if not)
refuses_when: package has no wbMonetize marker

wbRefactor.description: File-level structural refactor (split, extract, rename) without changing behavior.
wbRefactor.role: The Surgeon — structural surgery on a specific file.

example.description: Split monolith file into smaller pieces.

outputs.content: Refactor plan, before/after metrics, test impact.

fed_by: wbClean (ran first to remove dead code)

prerequisites: recent /wbAudit on the target file or its package
tests must currently PASS (otherwise no safety net)
refuses_when: test runner is broken (refactoring without tests is gambling)
/wbAudit not run on this file recently
notSuitableFor: Behavior changes (those are features, not refactors)

wbDebug.description: Form a hypothesis for an error before trying fixes.
wbDebug.role: The Diagnostician — root-cause analysis, not patch attempts.

input.content: Verbatim error message + optional stack trace

outputs.content: Hypothesis, evidence cited, suggested fix path with rationale.

prerequisites: the error must be an actual error message, not vague 'something is wrong'
refuses_when: no error message provided
notSuitableFor: Implementing the fix (do that after the diagnosis lands)
Vague problems without an error message (clarify the symptom first)

wbDoc.description: Generate or update documentation (README, JSDoc, API reference).
wbDoc.role: The Scribe — keeps docs in sync with code.

outputs.content: Updated README
Doc generation log, JSDoc additions, gaps flagged.

refuses_when: target has no exports to document

wbStandup.description: Consolidates unfinished work (open tickets, failing audits) into a single agenda.
wbStandup.role: The Scrum Master — cures choice paralysis.
wbStandup.belt: Black — only when juggling multiple in-flight workstreams

outputs.content: Open tickets grouped by package, audit debt, definitive next-command recommendation.

feeds_into: wbContext (standup recommends a package; context syncs it)
fed_by: wbAudit / wbReview / wbPlan (their reports become standup input)

prerequisites: at least some prior reports exist in target's reports/ tree
notSuitableFor: Real-time monitoring (run as scheduled cron, not interactive)

wbVision.description: Proposes 3 highly innovative, strategic features to build next.
wbVision.role: The Product Manager — generates feature ideas with rationale.

outputs.content: 3 feature ideas with 'Why it matters' and 'Complexity'.

feeds_into: wbPlan (when you commit to a vision)

notSuitableFor: Default 'nothing to do' command (close the laptop instead)

wbIdea.description: Captures, scores, and tracks speculative ideas. Promotes validated ideas to plan tasks via the Promotion Protocol.
wbIdea.role: The Ideator — scored idea pipeline between brainstorming and committed planning.
wbIdea.phase: Anytime (when you have an idea worth tracking but not committing to)

example.description: AI proposes scored ideas for wb-dataviewer.

example.command: /wbIdea packages/wb-core --task="Add retry logic to API calls"
example.description: Register a specific idea with computed score.
Promote idea #1 to today's plan file.

input.content: Context files, past audit/review reports, optional user description

outputs.content: Idea table with Score (1-10), Idea description, Priority, Est. Time, Suggested By, ☐ Done, ☐ Valid, → Task link.

feeds_into: wbPlan (promoted ideas auto-ingested as plan tasks)

prerequisites: ideally a prior /wbSetup, but not required
refuses_when: --promote on a 🚫 Rejected idea (must --open first)
notSuitableFor: Tasks ready for immediate execution (skip to /wbPlan or describe directly)

wbBroadcast.description: Generates an Announcement Kit and manages version lifecycle status.

example.description: Create announcement kit for wb-press2 release.

outputs.content: Social media drafts, GitHub release notes, blog post, VERSION_STATUS.md suggestion.

refuses_when: no recent successful release in cycle (announcing nothing = embarrassing)

wbGit.description: Analyzes current file changes and generates Conventional Commits.
wbGit.role: The Scribe (Git) — produces commit message text.
wbGit.phase: Midday + Evening (after every logical unit + after release tag)

example.description: Generate commit message for current working directory.

input.content: git status, git diff, recent plans/standups

outputs.content: Strict Conventional Commit message with optional body.

prerequisites: git working tree has changes (uncommitted or staged)
refuses_when: no changes to commit
user has feedback_no_git memory active (then output text only, never run git commands)
notSuitableFor: Branch operations (rebase, merge — use git directly)

wbSecure.description: Adversarial security scan: secrets, XSS, insecure deps, prototype pollution.
wbSecure.role: The Red Team — opposite hat from /wbAudit.

outputs.content: Findings categorized as CRITICAL, WARNING, SAFE.

fed_by: wbAudit (audit may delegate security questions to /wbSecure)

wbTranslate.description: Extracts hardcoded strings into i18n keys + EN/FR/AR JSON files.

example.description: Extract strings from Banner.vue + write fr.json + ar.json.

outputs.content: Summary of extracted keys.

prerequisites: i18n framework already wired in the target (vue-i18n)
refuses_when: target component has no hardcoded strings
notSuitableFor: Re-running on already-translated component (almost always a mistake)

wbToWBC.description: Rewrites legacy bulky HTML/Vuetify into proprietary WBC-UI2 JSON components.

example.description: Convert legacy table to wb-dataviewer JSON prop.

outputs.content: Rewritten component code, before/after line counts.

prerequisites: wbc-ui2 already a peer dep of the package
refuses_when: target file is already wbc-ui2 idiomatic

wbMonetize.description: Bootstraps or maintains the Free/Pro/Dev tier splitting plumbing.
wbMonetize.role: The Tier Architect — installs the freemium scaffolding.

example.description: Bootstrap with explicit split spec, no prompt.

outputs.content: Mode (bootstrap/maintenance/abort), split proposal, plumbing checklist, marker update.
wbMonetize marker injected

fed_by: wbVision (vision informs which features are Pro)

prerequisites: git working tree clean (recommended for bootstrap)
refuses_when: package has heuristic gating but no marker (ambiguous; abort with explanation)

wbActOn.description: Turns any diagnostic markdown (audit, plan, review, standup) into a ranked execution order with per-action recommended model.
wbActOn.role: The Triage Officer — converts diagnosis → prescription.
wbActOn.phase: After any /wb* report (or any third-party doc)

example.description: Process the audit; output a ranked execution thread.

outputs.content: Annotated source with [Model action] callouts, §0 Ranked Execution Order, action tally.

example.description: Find latest report under reports/ and act on it (asks if multiple).

prerequisites: the source file must have at least some structure (sections, tables, lists)
refuses_when: source has zero structure (free-form notes)
notSuitableFor: Executing the actions (run the prompts /wbActOn produced)
$design_notes: Outputs ALWAYS go to <target>/.wb/workflows/reports/, NEVER to docs/ai_reference/ (canon-only).
v2 Universal Daily File: action_<name>_<YYYYMMDD>.md and plan_<name>_<YYYYMMDD>.md — no model subfolder.
Multi-model: same source can be processed by N different models — each appends a new Entry #N to the same file, tagged *(ModelName — HH:MM)*. Diff entries to compare opinions.
Per-action model recommendation is mandatory: every callout includes a 'Recommended model: <name>' line.

morning_routine.description: Default daily startup: orient → load → triage

sequence.purpose: Sync AI on the chosen package

sequence.command: describe task in natural language OR /wbPlan

afternoon_polish.description: Code is done; polish before shipping.

sequence_classic.purpose: Only if audit flagged messy file

sequence_chained.purpose: Audit + ranked actions + plan in one run

evening_shipping_app.description: Ship an app to the live web

old_two_step.manual: Read findings, decide what to plan

audit_to_plan_chain.value_proposition: The chain forces the 5-color triage discipline BEFORE planning. Hand-run audit→plan often skips ranking. The chain produces a richer paper trail.
audit_to_plan_chain.fallback: Manual two-step remains valid when you want explicit control over what gets planned. Flags are convenience, not mandate.

$dependency_graph_text_view.$description: ASCII view of which commands feed into which, for chains and gates.
$graph: wbSecure ┼──► (gate: must PASS / no CRITICAL) ──► wbRelease ──► wbPublish ──► wbBroadcast
└──► (alt path for apps) ──► wbDeploy --target=local ──► wbDeploy ──► wbGit
wbTranslate / wbToWBC: cross-cutting one-shots (no chain dependencies)

$gates_and_refusals_summary.$description: Cross-command gates that prevent shipping mistakes. The harness should refuse these; if it doesn't, dev.md is missing the rule.

gates.refuses_if: no recent /wbRelease in the same cycle
dist/ vs dist-dev/ mismatch in package.json
no successful /wbRelease or /wbPublish in the same cycle
/wbAudit not run on this file recently OR test runner is broken
heuristic gating found without marker (ambiguous; needs --force-bootstrap)

$belt_progression.$description: Suggested learning order. See 2_daily_use/the_daily_playbook_claude.md for the canonical version.
$belt_progression.rule_that_beats_the_belt_system: If you only ever use /wbContext at session start, /wbAudit before shipping, and /wbGit to commit — you're already 80% of the way there.

wbCheck.description: Pre-Flight Context Quiz to verify worker model understanding.
wbCheck.role: The Quizmaster — verifies the AI actually understands the codebase before acting.

wbNext.description: Determine the immediate next optimal command to run based on codebase state.
wbNext.role: The Navigator — picks the next step when you're stuck.
wbNext.belt: White — first command when the path is unclear

wbTrack.description: Universal session logging — every subsequent /wb* invocation is appended to a walkthrough file with strategic commentary.
wbTrack.belt: Black — meta-command, used in long sessions

wbStopTrack.description: Stop session tracking, write final §END section, extract derivative files.

wbHelp.description: Print the role-grouped command catalog (no args) or one command's manual (`/wbHelp <wbX>` ≡ `/<wbX> -h`).

wbWork.description: Executes tasks defined in an active plan file.
wbWork.role: The Worker — writes code and produces execution reports.

outputs.content: Task execution report.

wbValid.description: Validates executed tasks against their original plan requirements.
wbValid.role: The Validator / QA — checks the Worker's code.

input.content: Active plan file and worker's task reports.

outputs.content: Appends PASS/FAIL findings to the worker's report.

wbExplain.description: Generate persistent, formatted explanations for a specific task ID, a codebase architecture, or a general technical question, without executing code changes.

example1.description: Explains task #1 from the plan using ELI5 style.

input.content: The plan markdown file containing the task

output.content: High-level summary, deep dive, and recommended approach.

example1.command: /wbExplain packages/wb-core/ "How does the Tier parser work?" --as=expert
example1.description: Deep dives into the architecture question for the wb-core package.

input.content: Source files relevant to the query

output.content: High-level summary, deep dive, and recommended approach.

--as.role: Adopts an exact tone or formatting style from shortcuts.md (e.g. eli5, expert, fr).

example1.description: Explains the task in French, using an ELI5 analogy.
